ts.reason: The JavaScript package runs Infomap in a single-threaded worker, so exposing OpenMP trial scheduling would be misleading.
The JavaScript package runs Infomap in a single-threaded worker, so exposing OpenMP node-move scheduling would be misleading.
The JavaScript package runs Infomap in a single-threaded WASM worker, so an OpenMP thread budget is meaningless there.
Alias of --num-threads; the single-threaded JavaScript worker has no OpenMP thread budget.
Distributed trial sharding is for multi-process HPC runs; the single-threaded JavaScript WASM worker cannot shard.
Per-shard result emission is for multi-process HPC merge workflows, not the JavaScript worker.
Only meaningful for shard runs feeding --merge-trial-results, which the JavaScript worker does not use.
